title: British vs American English
description: Key differences in spelling, vocabulary, pronunciation, and grammar between UK and US English.
summary_pt_br: Britânico vs americano: colour/color, lift/elevator, flat/apartment, have got/have.

scenes.subtitle_en: Key differences in spelling, vocabulary, pronunciation, and grammar between UK and US English.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Britânico vs americano: colour/color, lift/elevator, flat/apartment, have got/have.

scenes.text_en: Key differences in spelling, vocabulary, pronunciation, and grammar between UK and US English. Today's focus: british vs american english. In Britain we take the lift; in America they take the elevator.
scenes.text_pt: Britânico vs americano: colour/color, lift/elevator, flat/apartment, have got/have. Foco de hoje: british vs american english. No Reino Unido é lift; nos EUA é elevator.

scenes.title: British vs American English
scenes.body_en: Spelling: colour/color, centre/center. Vocabulary: lift/elevator, lorry/truck, flat/apartment. Grammar: have got/have, past participle differences.
scenes.body_pt: Britânico vs americano: colour/color, lift/elevator, flat/apartment, have got/have.

examples.en: In Britain we take the lift; in America they take the elevator.
examples.pt: No Reino Unido é lift; nos EUA é elevator.

examples.en: She coloured the drawing in blue.
examples.pt: Ela coloriu o desenho em azul.

examples.en: I've got three siblings. (UK) / I have three siblings. (US)
examples.pt: UK: I've got. US: I have.

scenes.title: Key vocabulary — British vs American English

exercise.question: Complete: "In Britain we take the lift; in America they take the ___."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "In Britain we take the lift; in America they take the elevator."
exercise.hint_pt_br: No Reino Unido é lift; nos EUA é elevator.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
She coloured the drawing in blue.
exercise.explanation: The correct sentence is: "She coloured the drawing in blue."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Ela coloriu o desenho em azul.

exercise.explanation: Five key terms from the lesson. Match each English word to its Portuguese meaning.

scenes.title: British vs American English — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre british vs american english. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: How do you say 'apartment' in British English?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: We say flat in the UK.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: It's a lorry in the UK.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Which sounds more natural: 'have got' or 'have'?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Both work, but have got is more British.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Is one variant 'correct' and the other wrong?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Language is a collection of dialects, not a hierarchy.

scenes.title: Two Englishes
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, detalhes e sua opinião. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Summarize three UK/US differences. Use at least two from each variant.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Resumo curto com quem falou, o que disseram e sua opinião.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Zara played two short recordings — one of a Londoner, one of a New Yorker — for her class to compare.

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: Notice that he says lift while she says elevator, yet both mean the same box going up and down.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: Is one spelling considered more correct than the other in international exams?

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: IELTS accepts both, but consistency throughout the text matters more than your choice of variant.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: So British and American English are equal, with different clothes on the same language?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: English, Zara told them, was never one garden — it was a forest grown across continents.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "British vs American English". Key differences in spelling, vocabulary, pronunciation, and grammar between UK and US English. In Britain we take the lift; in America they take the elevator. She coloured the drawing in blue. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: Prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "British vs American English". I liked it because it was clear and useful. See you next time!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule from last year
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "British vs American English".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para continuar praticando. Salve os links e volte sempre.
